records.title: Combined ratio by line of business
records.summary: Track combined ratio (loss ratio + expense ratio) by product line and geography to identify underwriting profitability trends and pricing adequacy.
records.metric_definition: Incurred losses plus underwriting expenses divided by earned premium.
records.methodology_note: Exclude reserve development from prior years for current-year view.

records.title: Claims settlement cycle time
records.summary: Measure average time from first notice of loss to claim settlement by claim type and severity band.
records.metric_definition: Median calendar days from FNOL to final payment by claim category.

records.title: Risk-based capital adequacy
records.summary: Maintain solvency margins above regulatory thresholds with transparent stress-testing of tail risks across all lines.

records.title: Claims data integrity and fraud prevention
records.summary: Ensure end-to-end data quality in the claims pipeline and deploy anomaly detection to identify suspicious patterns before payment.

records.title: Underwriting transformation through AI
records.summary: Augment underwriter decision-making with ML risk models, automated triage, and real-time portfolio steering to improve loss ratios while reducing cycle time.

records.title: Embedded insurance and ecosystem partnerships
records.summary: Embed insurance products at point of need within partner ecosystems to reduce acquisition cost and reach underserved segments.

records.title: Favor use cases that produce actuarially defensible outputs with model lineage
records.summary: Prioritize use cases whose outputs — loss predictions, pricing recommendations, reserve estimates — can be traced from input data through model version to final figure, satisfying regulatory actuarial sign-off requirements.

records.title: Unify underwriting, claims, and distribution data into shared risk views
records.summary: Prefer use cases that break down silos between underwriting, claims, and distribution by producing shared risk and performance views, enabling portfolio-wide decisions rather than function-specific analyses.
